reasoning: The reference class is the probability of a randomly selected individual stock's price exceeding its current price after periods of 1 week, 1 month, 3 months, and 6 months. Historically, due to the equity risk premium, this drifts slightly above 50% (typically 51-54%). The fetch_ts_yfinance tool returned a statistical expectation of P=0.56. Following a recent positive Q1 earnings release on April 29, the stock rebounded significantly (from ~$63 to ~$67) with several analysts maintaining "Hold" or moving price targets slightly higher into the mid-to-high $70s. AOS has also reaffirmed a $400M solid buyback program for 2025, which provides a price floor, leading to positive near-term post-earnings announcement drift (PEAD). By contrast, quarterly cash dividends (~$0.34 each in late July/October) will mechanically drag down the price return over the 3-mon and 6-mon horizons. Adhering to the prompt's instruction to make uncertainty increase and thus move longer-dated forecasts closer to 0.5, I gradually pull back toward 0.5 for the later dates.
